op.text: On March 14, 2025, scattered mother's ashes at Beacon Rock in the Columbia River Gorge; went alone; said 'Beacon Rock was ours — she used to take me there when I was a kid, and on the way up she taught me to read cloud shapes. Not just "that one's a rabbit" — she had this whole taxonomy, what a mare's tail sky meant, when the anvil clouds meant get off the water. I stood at the top and the sky was doing exactly the kind of high thin herringbone she loved, and I let her go into the wind.' Picked up a fir cone from the trail on the way down, now on windowsill above kitchen sink. Sam had soup waiting when Dana got home; slept eleven hours. (2025-03-18)
[as of 2025-03-18] In cutback week of marathon training as of 2025-03-18
